text:
Market today: Chip names are the story - a record $2T value added to semis in Q2, and that momentum is clearly lifting your SMH, SOXL, and SPAL. Sentiment in tech/chips is hot. Small-caps also had a monster H1, which supports the broader risk-on tone.

Your holdings: All positions are on HOLD with gains - nothing screaming danger right now. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF - on margin, a sharp reversal hits you twice as hard; watch that $200 stop closely. SPAL (+1.91%) is brand-new and very volatile; it moves fast and decays, so stay tight to $21.38. QQQI (+0.67%) is barely moving - fine as an income anchor but not doing heavy lifting.

Watch: (1) Whether the chip rally has legs or starts stalling after this massive Q2 run - extended moves reverse hard. (2) NVDA price action as a sector health check.

Bottom line: You're positioned right for this market, but your leveraged names (SOXL, SPAL) on margin are the one real risk - protect those stops.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it. On margin with a 3x leveraged ETF, a gap-down through that level is a serious hit; no heroics if it breaks.
SPAL: Brand-new and fast-moving - keep the $21.38 stop firm. Consider sizing down if the position feels large relative to your overall margin exposure.

No new buys today - scanner shows ALAB (9/12, entry 482.54-481.96, stop 479.73) as the cleanest setup, but with chips already up $2T in Q2 you're chasing; wait for a pullback entry to confirm rather than buying into extended momentum.
